name: Delaware Registered Construction Contractors Public Lookup
updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the public lookup before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: construction contractor registrations exposed by the Delaware public lookup
construction services and maintenance registrations under the Delaware Contractor Registration Act
knownExclusions: The public lookup is a construction contractor registry and does not replace Delaware professional, environmental, municipal, permit, or business-license requirements.
Electrical, plumbing, HVACR, and asbestos credentials are handled through separate Delaware licensing systems and must be preserved as companion sources.
Contractors absent from the registry may be under review, removed, suspended, revoked, or outside the registry scope.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Do not automate lookup traffic unless official terms and technical controls are reviewed.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license, registration, or authorization does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: The official page supports searches, including an all-contractors search, but no stable file or API export was exposed during the 2026-07-02 review.
researchNotes: The source is contractor-specific and backed by the Delaware Contractor Registration Act. It covers construction services broadly, but trade-specific professional credentials and environmental credentials remain separate companion sources.
maintainerNotes: Keep Delaware contractor registry scope separate from DELPROS professional-license records, DFM asbestos credentials, local permits, and Division of Revenue business licenses.

researchEvidence.note: Official search supports name, ZIP, certification number, NAICS, and all-contractors queries; no stable file or API contract was documented.

The Delaware Contractor Registration Act requires contractors to register before performing construction services or maintenance and defines construction services broadly across buildings, structures, utilities, excavating, and related work.

Official One Stop guidance lists related NAICS examples including general construction, roofing, plumbing, HVAC, sheet metal, sewer piping and storm drains, pipeline construction, asbestos removal, electrical, and mechanical construction.

blocker.summary: The contractor-specific search has no documented stable file or API interface, and its absence semantics require live agency confirmation.
